title: Functions as Values, Signatures, and Composition · Spiral Review
summary: Turn repeated validation branches into reusable transformations for an intake service. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a typed composition library with signature evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass, store, return, and inspect callables while preserving clear input-output contracts.
Model callable parameter and return relationships with precise type annotations.
Compose compatible unary transformations and explain data flow across boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the First Class Functions value-and-effect pipeline for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps First Class Functions assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Three intake records need normalize → validate → score transformations. A rejected record must retain its reason, and adding a fourth transformation must not change earlier function contracts.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so First Class Functions has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the First Class Functions precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: First-class functions execute as soon as referenced.

options.text: Separate immutable values from observable effects and ownership boundaries
